div.p: TECHNICAL INSIGHT: Sort-Merge Join is the optimal choice when inputs are already sorted (via Index Scans or previous operations). It avoids the memory overhead of Hash Join's build-phase and the O(N log N) penalty of explicit sorting.

g.text: while i < len(A) and j < len(B):
i += 1 # Logic handles M:1 or M:N

div.p: Step 1: A[0]=101, B[0]=101 -> MATCH -> emit Step 2: A[1]=101, B[0]=101 -> MATCH -> emit Step 3: A[2]=102, B[1]=102 -> MATCH -> emit Step 4: A[3]=103, B[2]=103 -> MATCH -> emit Complexity: O(M + N) Linear Scan

div.h2: Sort Left: O(M log M) [8 units] Sort Right: O(N log N) [5 units] Merge Phase: O(M + N) [7 units]
Sort Left: 0 (Already Sorted) Sort Right: 0 (Already Sorted) Merge Phase: O(M + N) [7 units]
